label: expensiveness
pattern: WhenTrue; BE-SPEC with VERY BIG surrogate for price
gloss: The quality of having a price greatly above the ordinary or expected amount for something of its kind, so that acquiring it requires an unusually large payment.
notes: AST adequacy: lossy — the rendering substitutes a very-big specification for a high monetary price because the metalanguage has no primitives for money, prices, or comparison of amounts.
